title: Induction without generalizing the accumulator leaves a too-weak IH
informal_statement: The accumulator-passing fold identity `l.foldl (fun a x => x :: a) acc = l.reverse ++ acc` for `l acc : List ℕ`, proved by `induction l` with `acc` fixed in context (not generalized), closing the `cons` case with `exact ih` alone.
formal_statement_pp: theorem root_theorem : ∀ (l : List ℕ) (acc : List ℕ), l.foldl (fun a x => x :: a) acc = l.reverse ++ acc

failure.input_context_summary: In the `cons x xs` case of `induction l` (after `intro l acc`, so `acc` is fixed before the induction), goal `List.foldl (fun a x => x :: a) acc (x :: xs) = (x :: xs).reverse ++ acc`; hypothesis `ih : List.foldl (fun a x => x :: a) acc xs = xs.reverse ++ acc`.
failure.attempt_summary: Closed the `cons` case with `exact ih` alone. Rejected with a clean type mismatch: `ih` proves the identity for `xs` with the SAME fixed `acc`, but the goal (after `List.foldl` unfolds one step) needs the identity for `xs` with accumulator `x :: acc`, an instance `ih` was never generalized to cover. kernel_fail (parse_error/type_mismatch, ~51s -- the slowest tactic-failure recorded so far in this corpus, likely from unification search before giving up).
failure.lesson: Because `acc` was introduced (fixed) before `induction l` ran, the induction hypothesis is only available for the ORIGINAL `acc`, not the `x :: acc` the successor case actually needs -- the textbook accumulator-generalization trap. The fix is `induction l generalizing acc` (or `intro l; induction l generalizing acc; intro acc`), which re-quantifies `acc` inside the IH so it applies at any accumulator, not just the one fixed before induction started. Contrast with the companion positive packet elementary.induction.foldl_cons_eq_reverse_append.v1, which proves the SAME root statement but closes each case with `simp [ih]` instead of `exact ih` -- simp's rewriting is strong enough to bridge the accumulator-shift gap that bare `exact` cannot, so the failure is specific to closing tactics that don't (or can't) rewrite through the mismatch, not to the statement itself.

notes: Produced via tracked proofsearch episode d5057978-41c6-4686-b360-a0947d8a518e, reusing the already-registered problem_version 204fecc3-8ba6-4fac-bf30-03dec9b20ad4 (same root statement as the companion positive packet elementary.induction.foldl_cons_eq_reverse_append.v1's episode 0ab12a3b): `solve` with `intro l acc; induction l with | nil => simp | cons x xs ih => exact ih` returned kernel_fail (type mismatch, exactly the predicted IH-too-weak shape), then closed with `give_up`. This is the corrected 'take 2' of the induction-without-generalizing hypothesis queued in this file: the first attempt (`simp [ih]` in the cons case) actually kernel-verified and became the positive packet instead of a negative example -- see that packet's notes and this domain's QUEUE.md history for the full story. Second negative example for the induction domain, alongside pow_succ_atom_nlinarith_failure.v1.
